title: Maker registry
description: The canonical-entities registry for this dataset (the archetype's `operators` slot). Makers and the parent companies that own them. Curated, low churn, cited. Every corporate-event claim (acquisition, closure, lineage) carries a source; anything unverified is named in `gaps` rather than left silently blank.

type.description: maker = builds the packs this dataset catalogs. parent-company = owns a maker; present so acquisitions resolve to a real entity rather than a string.

status.description: acquired means the maker still operates under an owner (see `parent`); it is a fact about ownership, not a verdict on the product.

acquired_date.description: The date the acquisition CLOSED, not the date it was announced. Record the announcement separately in notes if the two differ.

confidence.description: medium or low when the figure comes from business press rather than the filing itself.

predecessors.description: Prior brands in this maker's lineage, as history. Not ids unless those brands are themselves registered.

gaps.description: Named unknowns. A gap is a statement that we looked and did not find; an omission is a statement of nothing.

kind.description: primary = the maker or the acquirer speaking for itself. secondary = press, review, encyclopedia.
